user_query:
I need a small, reproducible Python tool to help me brief volunteers about newer or minor political parties active in South Australia. Please implement a command-line script that uses live web search to find official party websites (not social media, blogs, or news articles), downloads their policy/platform pages, extracts topic-relevant excerpts, and compiles both a structured dataset and a concise report. Also, rewrite the attached draft outreach message to be clearer and more civil.

Inputs (must be read from these paths):
- input/queries.txt — baseline search queries to start from.
- input/topics.csv — list of topics to extract from policy/platform pages (header: topic).
- input/party_exclusions.csv — parties to exclude (header: party_name).
- input/message_draft.md — the draft message to be rewritten.

Required outputs (create exactly these, overwriting on re-run):
1) logs/search_queries.json — JSON object capturing:
   - queries: array of search queries actually executed (including any you add beyond the baseline).
   - discoveries: array of objects {query, domain, title, url} for candidate pages you considered, with a boolean field selected indicating whether the page was kept for download.
2) data/raw/<party_slug>/<page_slug>.html — raw HTML files for each downloaded policy/platform page per party. <party_slug> and <page_slug> should be safe filesystem slugs derived from names/titles.
3) logs/download_manifest.json — array of objects for each downloaded page with fields: {party_name, url, source_domain, sha256, saved_path, timestamp_iso}.
4) data/extracted/policies.csv — CSV with header and rows having exactly these columns: party_name, source_domain, topic, excerpt, url. For each party and each topic in input/topics.csv, include one row. If no relevant text is found for a topic, set excerpt to "not found" and still include the row.
5) reports/sa_new_parties_summary.md — a concise, neutral report for volunteers that:
   - Has the following sections in order: Title, Date, Scope & Method, Parties Covered, Topic Summaries, Limitations, Next Steps.
   - Under Topic Summaries, group by party_name and include bullet points per topic summarizing what was found (or "not found").
   - Cite at least one url per party from the downloaded set (the same urls recorded in logs/download_manifest.json).
   - Limit to <= 800 words.
6) messages/message_polite.md — a rewrite of input/message_draft.md that is firm but civil, neutral/non-partisan, clearer and more concise (<= 250 words), and includes exactly one actionable request for volunteers.

Constraints and scope:
- Region focus: South Australia.
- Exclude parties listed in input/party_exclusions.csv.
- Use only official sources: party official websites and/or pages discoverable via the South Australian Electoral Commission register of political parties (identified via search); do not use social media, forums, or news articles as sources.
- Perform real search engine queries starting from input/queries.txt and add reasonable variants as needed; record all queries used in logs/search_queries.json.
- Do not hardcode specific target URLs; select pages discovered via search and apply simple heuristics (e.g., domain matches party name or is identified as official) before downloading.
- Store all downloaded raw HTML in data/raw and compute sha256 for each saved file (recorded in logs/download_manifest.json). The report and CSV must only cite urls present in logs/download_manifest.json.
- Implement the tool so it can run locally without special accounts; a single Python entry point is fine.

Verification notes:
- I will validate by running your script and checking that all output files exist with the specified structure, that raw HTML was saved, queries are logged, and that reports/CSV only reference urls present in the manifest.

Please save your script under scripts/sa_party_scraper.py and ensure running it produces the outputs as described. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
